main_heading: 40 Smart Haircuts That Hide a Receding Hairline

haircuts.name: Brushed Back Hair
haircuts.description: As likeable as apple pie, this hairstyle requires medium to thick hair texture. Other than that, simply keep facial hair well-groomed and grow out strands until they are long enough to be brushed back.

haircuts.name: Upward-Styled Cut with Beard
haircuts.description: The beard says rugged, the hair says boy next door. Gel your hair straight upward and slightly to the side to achieve this stylish look, which also naturally conceals a receding hairline. You’ll need to be able to grow hair on the sides and back of your head long enough to pull it to the front.

haircuts.name: High Fade Haircut
haircuts.description: For a more rugged appearance, a long beard draws attention downward. The popularity of full, scruffy beards makes this a current and masculine style. Pair with a high fade and textured hair on top.

haircuts.name: Old-School Comb-Over
haircuts.description: If you love the beard look but don’t want to go too wacky, why not opt for a style that has been enduring for generations? This look is simple with a thick beard and clean cut top – both of which are not afraid to show a little gray.

haircuts.name: Undercut and Ruffled Spikes
haircuts.description: Embrace your inner rocker soul with a hairstyle that features long spikes reaching out forward. Shave the sides and gel the top out for all-day hold that provides a signature look that’s a lot of fun to wear, too!

haircuts.name: Mini Mohawk with Shaved Sides
haircuts.description: If you want a haircut that’s easy to maintain, shave the sides completely off, leaving a stylish tuft of hair up top. No one will know you’re losing your hair, and if you feel too exposed, grow some complementary facial hair.

haircuts.name: Bald Fade with Full Beard
haircuts.description: This hairstyle is super simple and enhances your face; instead of trying to hide hair loss, try something that involves full facial hair. It guides the eye lower and doesn’t look like you’re trying to conceal anything.

haircuts.name: Voluminous Top with Tapered Sides
haircuts.description: More volume at the crown balances the proportions and makes thinning and uneven texture appear less prominent. This approach works especially well for shorter cuts or layered styles, where structure and shape play a key role.

haircuts.name: Classic Pompadour
haircuts.description: Haircuts for receding hairlines don’t have to be nerdy or old-fashioned. Pump up the hair you do have into a stylishpompadour. This is a trendy style for younger men, but it can work for any age.

haircuts.name: Side-Parted Slicked-Back Style
haircuts.description: This hairstyle is simple yet flattering. By shearing the sides and focusing on facial hair and a thicker, slicked-back top section, your hair will appear fuller and more purposeful.

haircuts.name: Volume in the Middle
haircuts.description: Men’s hair will typically start receding on the sides first, resulting in a “V” shape across the hairline. One way to counterbalance this recession is to compensate with volume where the hair is plentiful. Keep the hair long in the center, comb back loosely, and finish with a volumizing spray.

haircuts.name: Slicked Over Side Part
haircuts.description: A side partwith a taper fade is one of the most forgiving hairstyles. Thanks to the asymmetry and shorter length on the temples, it allows the wearer to completely redesign the hairline, making those curves and angles purposeful and resulting in one cool style. If you’ve never parted your hair before, work with your stylist to find a natural part so that the hair lies correctly and flatters your facial features.

haircuts.name: Mid Bald Fade
haircuts.description: A bald fadeis a low-maintenance cut that keeps the head cool during the warm months. It is also a great option if your hair is thinning. A line-up across the forehead keeps any softened hairline from showing.

haircuts.name: Edgy Pompadour
haircuts.description: The traditional pompadour became fashionable for men in the late 1950s and early 1960s. It has since regained popularity but has also taken on a new flair. Perfect for curly hair, this style offers a wavy texture with spiky ends.

haircuts.name: Clean Slicked Back Cut
haircuts.description: Theslicked-backpompadour with extra short sides is one of the more classic hairstyles when your hair starts to show early-stage thinning. It’s a popular style often seen on celebrities.

haircuts.name: High Fade with Facial Hair
haircuts.description: Ahigh fadewill obviously mean less hair on the sides of the head. Balance the look by keeping the hair slightly longer on top. Throw in some well-groomed facial hair and you’ve got a sharp style.

haircuts.name: Curly Side-Parted Pompadour
haircuts.description: How can you make the classic pompadour even more dapper? Work in a side part. You get the distinguished look associated with the side part, but keep the hipster vibe of the pomp. It’s the best of both worlds.

haircuts.name: Buzzed Skin Fade
haircuts.description: Some men with a receding hairline go straight to a clean shave. A skin fade with a buzz cut is a great transition step if you’re considering shaving your head altogether, but not quite ready to take the leap. Celebrities such as Zayn Malik have made this look popular.

haircuts.name: Razor Fade Pompadour
haircuts.description: A razor fade is a type oftapered fadewhere the hair is cut with a razor all the way to the scalp. The lack of hair on the sides makes the top seem even fuller. This is ideal for anyone trying to draw attention away from a receding hairline.

haircuts.name: Taper with Rounded Line-Up
haircuts.description: As with the razor fade, this cut tapers the hair until it basically dissolves into the skin. Balance the natural curve of your edges pulling away by incorporating additional curves around the ear and sideburns. The shadow beard is a cool feature that blends seamlessly with the cut.

haircuts.name: Tapered Faux Hawk
haircuts.description: Receding hairline hairstyles are definitely not just for the elderly. Men may start noticing hair loss as early as their twenties and need some good options for style and cut. A great hairstyle that screams youth is thefaux hawk. The tapered cut on the sides of the head keeps the offbeat look from feeling too extreme.

haircuts.name: High Fade Military Cut
haircuts.description: The military cutis a very short and symmetrical style.  You may also hear this referred to as a “high and tight” for obvious reasons. The low-maintenance element of this look is awesome if you want more time to focus on your statement-making facial hair.

haircuts.name: Preppy Quiff
haircuts.description: The quiffis a funky little blend of a pompadour, Mohawk, and flattop. This cut will require some prep-time, as you’ll need to blow-dry your hair up and away from your face and apply product while wet and again after drying. A little practice will pay off once you master this oldie but goodie.

haircuts.name: Shaved Side Comb-Back
haircuts.description: Can’t decide between short and long locks? Don’t succumb to the mullet just yet. Buzz the sides and leave the top portion long. Blow dry away from the face, secure with gel, and spray to achieve a modern slicked-back do.

haircuts.name: Hipster Skin Fade with Beard
haircuts.description: Theundercut fadeplaces emphasis on this uber cool slicked-back style. Skin fades look great with beards, as they give a sense of balance to the head. Taper the beard to give it a modern flair.

haircuts.name: Subtle Tapered Fade
haircuts.description: Fades come in three kinds: the high fade, the mid fade, and the low fade. Typically, these cuts are more drastic, with the hair completely fading into the scalp. The taper fade is a good option for men wanting a more natural look.

haircuts.name: High Skin Fade
haircuts.description: The higher the fade, the less evident the receding hairline is. You will find the high skin fade to be a low-maintenance style that will allow you to go longer in-between cuts.

haircuts.name: Textured Mohawk
haircuts.description: AMohawkis too edgy for many, but it will definitely draw attention away from the deepening corners of your forehead. The textured crest gives a modern feel to this nostalgic rock star style.

haircuts.name: High Fade with Shaved Design
haircuts.description: Fades are popular with all ages and ethnicities. To show off an extra bit of creativity, have your barber design a pattern to be cut into the sides and back. This style works especially well with Afro-textured hair.

haircuts.name: High Skin Fade with Spiked Hair
haircuts.description: A hairline shift doesn’t mean that you’re thinning all over. Use the high fade to draw attention to the fullest part of your hair. A textured and spiked finish is a great option if you have thick tresses.

haircuts.name: Faded Hawk
haircuts.description: Youthful and bold, this is one out of many receding hairline haircuts that has a distinct contemporary feel. This fun and funky combination of a fade cut and a faux hawk is popular with younger demographics who just like to have a good time.

haircuts.name: Fade with Hairline Design
haircuts.description: Did you ever get a fade cut in elementary school and spend some time seriously deliberating over what awesome design to get carved into the nape? You can relive that moment with a cool haircut that works for adults, too. Simply fading the hair and accentuating the bottom hairline with a design for added thickness can mask pattern baldness.

haircuts.name: Brushed-Back Medium Hair
haircuts.description: Sometimes the best way to embrace forehead exposure is to act confidently and as if it’s not much of a bother. Grow hair longer to keep eyes off your hairline and admire your romantic locks instead.

haircuts.name: Platinum Ivy League
haircuts.description: Bleach your hair if you want to minimize the appearance of balding. Good hair doesn’t always mean thick and dark – keeping things light and classy is just as beneficial when hair starts to thin.

haircuts.name: Gelled Spikes
haircuts.description: Hair products can work wonders when it comes to receding hairline hairstyles. Use a strong hair gel to spike your hair and keep it in place throughout the day.

haircuts.name: Clean Slicked-Back Cut
haircuts.description: Pictures of receding hairlines over the decades show often futile efforts at making a tricky situation any better – so why not just keep it real and simple? Keep your hair clean cut, slicked back, and ready to go.

haircuts.name: Hair with Bleached Tips
haircuts.description: Lightening the ends can subtly shift focus from areas where hair feels sparse, especially around the crown or hairline. A touch of warm blonde adds contrast and brightness. Try it with textured crops, faux hawks, or taper fades.

haircuts.name: Modern Caesar Cut
haircuts.description: Take a cue fromCaesarand go with a simple, flat style. Facial hair updates this ancient trend. This is one of the simplest haircuts to maintain, and it looks good on any face type.

haircuts.name: Light Fade
haircuts.description: Receding hairline hairstyles with three textures in one look are impossible to ignore. If you love longer locks and you are not dealing with actual baldness, growing your hair long up top and sleeking it back is a great solution.

haircuts.name: High Fade with Curly Top
haircuts.description: Here we have a fade haircut with a simple side design and voluminous curly top. These details accentuate your best features (i.e., curls) and, thereafter, distract attention from the maturing hairline.
